questions.question: Why model the harness loop as an explicit state machine rather than a while loop with flags?
options: It lets the harness reject illegal transitions in one placešłéreplay sessions from the event log
It is faster on CPython
It avoids the need for tests
It is the only shape the model can call
questions.explanation: A state machine puts the legality rules in one transition function. The same input sequence reproduces the same state, which is what makes session replay possible.

questions.question: What does a pull point in the loop represent?
options: A point where the loop yields controlšłéwaits for the caller to provide an input
A model call that returns a tool name
A timer that fires every N seconds
A hook subscriber that mutates payload
questions.explanation: Pull points are returns, not exceptions. The caller fetches what the harness asked foršłécalls resume().

questions.question: Which state is the only legal pull point for a tool call?
options: EXECUTING
AWAITING_TOOL
REFLECTING
PLANNING
questions.explanation: Only AWAITING_TOOL yields a tool pull point. EXECUTING transitions into it when a step requires a tool.

questions.question: What happens when a hook raises HookAbort in before_tool_call?
options: The session immediately transitions to DONE
The tool call is skipped, on_error fires,šłéthe loop moves to REFLECTING
The hook is unregistered
The budget is reset
questions.explanation: HookAbort cancels the in-flight tool dispatch, fires on_error with the abort reason,šłécontinues.

questions.question: Why are hooksšłéevents kept as separate concerns?
options: Because event types are bytesšłéhooks are strings
Hooks are imperativešłécan mutate or abort. Events are observationalšłéappend-only.
Because the audit script forbids subscribers
Because the event log cannot store dicts
questions.explanation: Hooks change behavior. Events describe behavior. Mixing them couples policyšłételemetry, which is the bug we are avoiding.

questions.question: When the wall-clock budget is exceeded, what is the next state?
options: DONEÔľöthe session is terminated
EXECUTINGÔľöthe loop retries
IDLEÔľöa pull point is returned with a budget_exceeded reason
PLANNINGÔľöa fresh plan is drafted
questions.explanation: Budget exhaustion is a yield. The harness transitions to IDLE, fires on_budget_exceeded,šłéreturns a PullRequest so the caller can decide whether to extend or close.

questions.question: Why does this lesson stub out the planner instead of calling a model?
options: Because models are slow to call locally
To keep the loop contract testablešłédeterministic before any model is bound
Because models cannot return tool calls
To save tokens
questions.explanation: Pinning the loop contract first lets later lessons swap in real planners, registries,šłédispatchers without renegotiating the state machine.
